response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang backgrounds, but the role demands deep embedded networking security skills that are absent: no evidence of C for UNIX beyond a brief stint at National Instruments, no Linux kernel analysis or modification experience, no MACsec/802.1X/authentication or L2/L3 switch development, and critically no Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native-level). The mismatch on core required qualifications makes this a poor fit despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and systems
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux proficiency
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, an explicit hard requirement
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
No Linux kernel investigation/modification experience; recent work is web/full-stack, far from firmware

key_factors.factor: General seniority and systems programming exposure
